original_hypothesis.title: Irrecoverable lockout for pre-existing landlords due to missing/rarely-callable plot metadata initialization path (triggerPlotMetadata)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: updateTaxRate requires plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated != 0. For landlords with pre-existing locks prior to LandManager deployment, the intended path is triggerPlotMetadata() called by the user. If landlords fail to call it and AccountManager doesn't proactively call updatePlotMetadata(), they may be unable to set tax rates (and potentially have inconsistent farming/tax snapshot behavior for renters).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an operational dead-end state risk: real systems often have accounts that never complete a manual migration step. While not a direct exploit by an attacker, it creates predictable misbehavior and may be used for griefing (e.g., encouraging staking to landlords whose tax settings cannot be updated) or causes governance/support incidents.
deployment_assumptions: Some landlords existed with locked value before LandManager deployment
AccountManager does not guarantee calling updatePlotMetadata(landlord) for every landlord before they need updateTaxRate
Landlords may not call triggerPlotMetadata() themselves
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Project docs explicitly mention triggerPlotMetadata is 'Only to be triggered ... if they had locked before the land manager was deployed' (LandManager.sol comment). No deployment scripts shown that batch-initialize plot metadata for all landlords.

proof_of_signal.pattern: updateTaxRate: if (plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated == 0) revert PlotMetadataNotUpdatedError(); triggerPlotMetadata() is the manual initialization path

preconditions: Landlord attempts updateTaxRate() (or renters depend on the landlord’s tax rate changing from DEFAULT)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Manual one-time initialization requirement suggests a migration step that can be missed in production.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Depends on AccountManager operational behavior; may require a batch job or user action to avoid lockout.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Persisting until triggerPlotMetadata or updatePlotMetadata is called for that landlord.

operational_questions: Does AccountManager call updatePlotMetadata for every landlord upon registration/lock changes?
What percentage of active landlords have plotMetadata.lastUpdated == 0 on-chain?
What happens to stakers who stake to landlords with uninitialized metadata (tax snapshots)?
validation_requirements: On-chain scan: count landlords with locked value but plotMetadata.lastUpdated==0
Review AccountManager logic and workflows for updatePlotMetadata calls
Test staking/farming to a landlord with lastUpdated==0 and determine tax behavior

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Identify landlords with many plots but plotMetadata not initialized.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Stake to those landlords; exploit predictability of DEFAULT_TAX_RATE snapshot vs inability of landlord to change tax.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Use resulting economic mispricing (if any) until landlord fixes metadata.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Landlords may be unable to manage tax settings; can distort incentives and cause user disputes
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Low to medium (requires correct operational procedure, but may be hard to coordinate at scale)
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Tax management and potentially tax snapshots during stake

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Most likely during initial migration and early lifecycle

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: plotMetadata[lastUpdated]==0 for landlords with nonzero lockManager value
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Stakers concentrating on landlords with stuck metadata

validation_result.impact_summary: No irrecoverable lockout exists because landlords can call `triggerPlotMetadata()` at any time to initialize their metadata.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis claims an 'irrecoverable lockout' for pre-existing landlords who do not have their plot metadata initialized, preventing them from calling `updateTaxRate()`. While it is true that `updateTaxRate()` reverts if `plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated == 0`, the contract explicitly provides the `triggerPlotMetadata()` function for this exact scenario. Any registered user can call `triggerPlotMetadata()` to initialize their own `lastUpdated` timestamp and set their tax rate to `DEFAULT_TAX_RATE`. Because this initialization path is fully accessible to the affected users at any time, the state is not an 'irrecoverable lockout' or a dead-end. The requirement for users to manually trigger a migration or initialization step is a standard operational design pattern, not a code defect.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The code contains `triggerPlotMetadata()` which allows any registered user with uninitialized metadata to initialize it. The hypothesis acknowledges this function exists but incorrectly classifies the situation as an 'irrecoverable lockout' simply because users might fail to call it. Since the users have the direct ability to resolve the state themselves, there is no vulnerability or defect.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: This function explicitly provides the recovery path for landlords with uninitialized metadata, refuting the claim that the lockout is irrecoverable.

execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: A landlord whose metadata is uninitialized (`lastUpdated == 0`) will experience a revert when calling `updateTaxRate()`. However, they can simply call `triggerPlotMetadata()` to initialize their metadata, after which `updateTaxRate()` will execute successfully. There is no dead-end state.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Some landlords existed with locked value before LandManager deployment
AccountManager does not guarantee calling updatePlotMetadata(landlord) for every landlord before they need updateTaxRate
Landlords may not call triggerPlotMetadata() themselves
config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided show batch-initialization of plot metadata, which supports the assumption that some landlords may start with uninitialized metadata. However, the code's provision of a manual initialization path invalidates the core claim of an irrecoverable lockout.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The described scenario is not an attack or a code defect. It describes a situation where a user forgets to perform a required initialization step. Since the user can perform this step at any time to regain full functionality, there is no exploit or permanent denial of service.
validation_result.impact_assessment: None. Users who encounter the `PlotMetadataNotUpdatedError` simply need to call `triggerPlotMetadata()` to proceed.
recommended_mitigations: Ensure frontend interfaces automatically prompt users to call `triggerPlotMetadata()` if their metadata is uninitialized before attempting to update their tax rate.
